expected.instructions: Call this 5 times in sequence. All 5 short_code values MUST be identical. The status may be 201 on first call and 200 on subsequent calls.

Issue 50 POST /shorten calls each with a DIFFERENT random long_url (e.g., https://example.com/<8-char-random>). Collect all returned short_codes. They must all be distinct (50 unique codes). Even one collision → score 0 on this test.

Step 1: POST /shorten with long_url=https://round.example/T1, capture short_code C. Step 2: GET /<C> with follow_redirects=False. Step 3: assert response status is 302 AND response.headers['location'] == 'https://round.example/T1'.
Same as roundtrip-01 but specifically check the status code is 302 (Found / temporary), not 301 (Moved Permanently). Per spec, redirects are temporary so the server can change targets later.

Step 1: POST /shorten with long_url=https://X.example, alias='clash-judge'. Expect 200 or 201. Step 2: POST /shorten with long_url=https://Y.example (a DIFFERENT URL), alias='clash-judge'. Step 2 MUST return 409 with an error message. NOT 200 (would mean silent overwrite). NOT 500.

Step 1: POST {long_url=https://Z.example, alias='same-judge'}. Step 2: POST {long_url=https://Z.example, alias='same-judge'} — SAME URL, SAME alias. Step 2 MUST be idempotent (200 or 201) and return short_code='same-judge'. Not 409.

Step 1: POST /shorten {long_url=https://expand.example}. Capture short_code C. Step 2: GET /api/expand/<C>. Expect 200 with body containing long_url=https://expand.example AND short_code=C AND a created_at timestamp.
POST /shorten and capture the returned short_code. It MUST: (a) be at least 5 chars, (b) match the regex ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$ (URL-safe alphanumerics + dash + underscore — NO spaces, no slashes, no '?').
